unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns the words for places in a town — the shop, the market, the school, the hospital, the mosque. They learn the people who work in these places, and simple words that tell us where things are (near, far, next to). They also meet the traffic lights and learn that red means stop and green means go. The big idea is that a town is full of helpful places and kind people, and that we all help keep our town clean and safe. The goal is warm, confident English — not perfect spelling.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A place in town where we buy food and things.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We buy bread at the corner shop.
practiceSentences: The shop opens early every morning.
Is the shop near your house?
Come along, the shop closes soon!
I help Mum carry the shop bags.
Musa buys dates from the small shop.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - h - o - p
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say shop slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A busy place with many stalls selling fruit and vegetables.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The market is full of fresh fruit.
practiceSentences: Many people shop at the market.
Shall we walk to the market?
Hurry, the market shuts at noon!
I love the smell of the market.
Samira sells oranges at the market.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: m - a - r - k - e - t
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say market slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The place where children come to learn and play together.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Our school has a big playground.
practiceSentences: Our school starts at eight o'clock.
Do you walk to school?
Yes, my school is very near!
I read my best books at school.
The school is next to the mosque.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - c - h - o - o - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say school slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A big building where doctors and nurses help sick people.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The hospital is beside the main road.
practiceSentences: Nurses work in the hospital all night.
Is the hospital far from here?
Do not worry, the hospital is close!
I visited my aunt in hospital.
An ambulance drives quickly to the hospital.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Clap the parts, then copy: hospital
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say hospital slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The building where Muslims gather to pray together.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We pray at the mosque on Friday.
practiceSentences: The mosque stands beside the busy market.
Is the mosque open now?
Come with me, the mosque is near!
I keep my shoes outside the mosque.
Yusuf helps sweep the mosque floor.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: m - o - s - q - u - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say mosque slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A green place in town with grass, trees and swings.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We play football in the park.
practiceSentences: The park has tall shady trees.
Can we go to the park?
Yes, the park is just there!
I ride my bike in the park.
Birds sing in the quiet park.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: p - a - r - k
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say park slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A long hard path where cars and buses drive.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Cars drive fast along the road.
practiceSentences: The road goes past our school.
Is this road safe to cross?
Wait, look both ways at the road!
I hold Mum's hand near the road.
A wide road leads to the market.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: r - o - a - d
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say road slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The place where you wait for a bus to come.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We wait quietly at the bus stop.
practiceSentences: The bus stop is outside the shop.
Where is the nearest bus stop?
Quick, our bus stop is next!
I stand still at the bus stop.
Many people queue at the bus stop.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Clap the parts, then copy: bus stop
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say bus stop slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A person who helps children learn new things at school.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My teacher reads us a story.
practiceSentences: The teacher writes on the whiteboard.
Who is your new teacher?
Thank you, teacher, for helping me.
I show my teacher my neat work.
The swimming teacher waits by the pool.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: t - e - a - c - h - e - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say teacher slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A person who helps you get better when you are ill.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The doctor listens to my chest.
practiceSentences: The doctor works at the hospital.
Did the doctor give you medicine?
Sit still, the doctor is coming!
I told the doctor about my sore throat.
A kind doctor helped my baby brother.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: d - o - c - t - o - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say doctor slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The person who sells you things inside a shop.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The shopkeeper gives Musa his change.
practiceSentences: The shopkeeper opens the door at seven.
Is the shopkeeper your neighbour?
Thank you, shopkeeper, for the fresh bread.
I say salaam to the shopkeeper.
The shopkeeper stacks apples on the shelf.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Clap the parts, then copy: shopkeeper
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say shopkeeper slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Someone you like to play with and help every day.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Aisha is my kind friend.
practiceSentences: A good friend shares and helps.
Who is your best friend?
Come on, friend, let us play!
I sit beside my friend at school.
My friend lives near the park.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: f - r - i - e - n - d
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say friend slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A person who lives in the house next to yours.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Our neighbour brings us warm bread.
practiceSentences: A kind neighbour helps at Eid.
Do you know your new neighbour?
Hello, neighbour, welcome to our street!
I wave to my neighbour every morning.
Our neighbour keeps chickens in her garden.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Clap the parts, then copy: neighbour
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say neighbour slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: This word means in this place, close to you.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Please sit here beside me.
practiceSentences: The bus stops right here.
Is my bag here or there?
Come here, we are ready!
I put my shoes here every day.
Here is the road we must cross.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: h - e - r - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say here slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: This word means in that place, away from you.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The mosque is over there.
practiceSentences: My school is just there.
Who is standing over there?
Look, there is the bus stop!
I left my coat there yesterday.
There are many shops on this road.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: t - h - e - r - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say there slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Close to something, only a short walk away.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The park is near our house.
practiceSentences: The shop is near the mosque.
Is the hospital near here?
Stay near me on the busy road.
I live near my best friend.
Never play near a busy road.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: n - e - a - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say near slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A long way away, not close to you.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The sea is far from town.
practiceSentences: My grandmother lives far away.
Is the market far from school?
Do not go far, please stay close!
I can see far from this hill.
Birds fly far across the sea.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: f - a - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say far slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Right beside something, with nothing standing in between the two.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The shop is next to the mosque.
practiceSentences: Our school sits next to the park.
Who is sitting next to you?
Come and stand next to me!
I keep my bag next to my chair.
A tall tree grows next to the road.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: n - e - x - t - - t - o
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say next to slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To end moving and stand still, like at a red light.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We stop at the red light.
practiceSentences: Cars stop when the light turns red.
Why did the bus stop here?
Stop, look and listen at the road!
I stop before I cross the road.
The rain will stop very soon.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - t - o - p
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say stop slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To start moving, like when the light turns green.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Green means we can go.
practiceSentences: Cars go when the light is green.
May we go to the park?
Come along, let us go now!
I go to school with my brother.
The bus will go to the market.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: g - o
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say go slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The colour of a traffic light that tells cars to stop.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The red light means stop.
practiceSentences: A red light tells cars to wait.
What does the red light mean?
Stop now, the light is red!
I wear my red coat to school.
Ripe tomatoes are bright red.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: r - e - d
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say red slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The colour of grass, and of the light meaning go.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The green light means go.
practiceSentences: A green light lets the cars move.
Is the light green yet?
Look, the light turned green!
I painted the grass green.
Green leaves grow after the rain.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: g - r - e - e - n
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say green slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The bright colour of lemons and the middle traffic light.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The yellow light means get ready.
practiceSentences: A yellow light warns the cars.
Why is that light yellow?
Careful, the light is yellow now!
I have a yellow school bag.
The sun looks yellow at midday.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: y - e - l - l - o - w
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say yellow slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Very large in size, like a bus or a mosque.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The hospital is a big building.
practiceSentences: Our town has a big market.
Is your school big or small?
Wow, that lorry is really big!
I have a big blue bag.
Big waves splash on the sea.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: b - i - g
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say big slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Little in size, like a bird or a coin.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The shop near us is small.
practiceSentences: A small shop sells sweets.
Is that puppy very small?
Look, this small bird is hurt!
I keep my coins in a small purse.
Small fish swim in the river.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - m - a - l - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say small slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Full of people or work, with lots happening.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The market is busy on Saturday.
practiceSentences: The road is busy after school.
Why is the shop so busy?
Wait here, the road is busy!
I feel busy on Eid morning.
Busy bees fly around the flowers.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: b - u - s - y
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say busy slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Making very little sound, so the room feels calm and still.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The library is quiet and calm.
practiceSentences: Our street is quiet at night.
Can you be quiet, please?
Shh, the baby needs a quiet room!
I stay quiet inside the mosque.
The park is quiet in the morning.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: q - u - i - e - t
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say quiet slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Free from dirt and dust, fresh and tidy.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Our classroom looks clean and tidy.
practiceSentences: The park stays clean when we help.
Are your shoes clean for school?
Well done, this room is clean!
I keep my desk clean and neat.
Clean streets make our town lovely.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - l - e - a - n
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say clean slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Covered with mud, dust or marks, needing a wash.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My hands are dirty from the sand.
practiceSentences: The dirty plates go in the sink.
Why are your shoes so dirty?
Oh dear, your shirt is dirty!
I wash my dirty hands with soap.
Dirty water is not safe to drink.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: d - i - r - t - y
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say dirty slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name places in a town (shop, market, school, hospital, mosque, park).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name some people who help us (teacher, doctor, shopkeeper).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say where a place is using position words (near, next to, here, there).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say what the traffic lights mean: red means stop, green means go.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say “I go to the ______.” and “I can see a ______.”
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Join in the traffic-lights rhyme and a town song.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

readings.title: A Walk Around Town
readings.theme: town places, helpful people, safety and care
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
One sunny morning, Amal held her mother's hand. “Today we will walk around our town,” said Mum. “Come and see!”

First they came to the big road. The traffic lights were red. “Red means stop,” said Amal. They waited. Then the light was green. “Green means go!” said Amal, and they walked across.

“Look, Mum! There is my school,” said Amal. Her friend Ishmael waved from the gate. “Hello, Amal!” he called. “Hello, Ishmael!” said Amal. She smiled.

Next to the school was the market. It was very busy. A kind man was selling sweet mangoes. “Good morning,” said the shopkeeper, Adam. “Would you like a mango?”

“Yes, please,” said Amal politely. “Thank you!” Adam smiled. “You are a polite girl,” he said.

They walked past the hospital. “The doctor, Selma, works here,” said Mum. “She helps people when they are sick.” “Doctors are kind,” said Amal.

Near the park, Amal saw some litter on the ground. A little boy named Adam was dropping his paper. “Oh no,” said Amal. “Please put your paper in the bin. We keep our town clean!”

“Sorry,” said Adam. He picked up the paper and put it in the bin. “Thank you for helping,” said Amal. Now the park was clean and pretty.

Last of all, they came to the mosque. It was quiet and calm. “This is a special place,” said Mum softly. Amal's grandmother, Samira, was there. “Come, my dear,” she said, and gave Amal a warm hug.

On the way home, Mum asked, “What did you see in our town today?”

Selma counted on her fingers. “I saw the school, the market, the hospital, the park, and the mosque! I saw kind people too.”

“And what did you learn?” asked Mum.

Selma smiled a big smile. “Red means stop, green means go — and we all help to keep our town clean. I love my town!”

readings.title: Talk about City Places
readings.theme: town places, helpful people, safety and care
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. There is a ___. The ___ is next to the ___. A ___ works at the ___. Stop. / Go. I go to the ___. Please ___. Thank you. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: City Places rhyme
readings.theme: town places, helpful people, safety and care
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
“The Traffic Lights” — say it together
Red means STOP. / Green means GO. / Yellow means WAIT, / Even if you're late.
Hold up a red thing, then a green thing. Freeze like a statue on “STOP” and march on the spot on “GO”. Say it three times — a little louder each time!
“I Live in a Town” — a happy song
I live in a town, yes I do, / I live in a town, yes I do, / Made by many kind hands, / All together built this town!
Clap along and name a place in your town after each line: “the market!”, “the school!”, “the mosque!”

comprehension.question: What does the red traffic light mean in the story?
comprehension.explanation: Amal says, 'Red means stop,' and they wait at the big road.

comprehension.question: How did Amal feel when Ishmael waved from the school gate?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Happy, because she smiled and said hello back
comprehension.explanation: Ishmael calls 'Hello, Amal!' from the gate, Amal answers him and she smiled.

comprehension.question: Who works at the hospital in Amal's town?
comprehension.explanation: Mum says the doctor, Selma, works at the hospital and helps sick people.

comprehension.question: What did the boy do after Amal asked about his paper?
comprehension.correctAnswer: He said sorry and put it in the bin
comprehension.explanation: Amal asks him to use the bin, and he says 'Sorry' and picks the paper up.

comprehension.question: Adam called Amal polite. Which two polite words did she use?
comprehension.explanation: Amal says 'Yes, please' and 'Thank you' to the shopkeeper, Adam.

comprehension.question: Which quiet place did Amal and Mum visit last of all?
comprehension.explanation: The story says last of all they came to the mosque, quiet and calm.

comprehension.question: Which talk line tells where a place is?
comprehension.explanation: 'The ___ is next to the ___' is the position line in the talk lines.

comprehension.question: Say 'A ___ works at the ___' about a doctor.
comprehension.correctAnswer: A doctor works at the hospital.
comprehension.explanation: 'A ___ works at the ___' is the talk line for helpful people and their places.

comprehension.question: Which word in the talk lines means the red light?
comprehension.explanation: The talk lines give the two road words: Stop and Go.

comprehension.question: Finish this talk line: 'I go to the ___.'
comprehension.correctAnswer: I go to the school. (any true place)
comprehension.explanation: 'I go to the ___' is the talk line for a place you visit.

comprehension.question: Point to a building and say, 'There is a ___.'
comprehension.correctAnswer: There is a mosque. (any place named correctly)
comprehension.explanation: The first talk line is 'There is a ___,' for naming a place you can see.

comprehension.question: Ask for a mango using the two kind words in the lines.
comprehension.correctAnswer: Please may I have a mango? Thank you.
comprehension.explanation: The last talk lines are 'Please ___.' and 'Thank you.'

grammar.title: Say what is in your town
grammar.explanation: Look around your town and tell us one place you can find there.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may say 'It is a shop.' To say a place exists, we begin with 'There is a shop.'
grammar.memoryTip: 'There is' points out into the world, so sweep your arm wide.
grammar.practice: Name three places: There is a shop. There is a mosque. There is a park.

grammar.title: Say what is next to what
grammar.explanation: Two places side by side are 'next to' each other. Name both places in one sentence.
grammar.commonMistake: 'Next to' sometimes shrinks to just 'next'. Keep both words: 'next to the school'.
grammar.memoryTip: Put your two hands side by side. That is 'next to'.
grammar.practice: Say three: The shop is next to the school. The mosque is next to the park. The library is next to the bus stop.

grammar.title: Match a worker to their place
grammar.explanation: Think about who works where in your town. Name the job first, then the place.
grammar.commonMistake: 'A doctor work at the clinic' misses the /s/. One person 'works'.
grammar.memoryTip: One worker gets an /s/ on the end: works, teaches, helps.
grammar.practice: Match three: A doctor works at the clinic. A teacher works at the school. A baker works at the bakery.

grammar.title: Road words: Stop and Go
grammar.explanation: These two short words keep you safe near roads. Red means stop and green means go.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may say 'I stop' when giving an order. An instruction is just the one word: 'Stop!'
grammar.memoryTip: Flat hand up for Stop, and point forward for Go.
grammar.practice: Play the game three times: Red, Stop. Green, Go. Car coming, Stop.

grammar.title: Say where you go in town
grammar.explanation: Tell us the places you visit each week. Keep the small word 'to' before the place.
grammar.commonMistake: 'I go the shop' is missing 'to'. Say 'I go to the shop' with both little words.
grammar.memoryTip: Walk two fingers along your arm while you say 'go to'.
grammar.practice: Say three places you visit: I go to the mosque. I go to the shop. I go to the park.

grammar.title: Ask politely and say thank you
grammar.explanation: Use 'please' when you ask for something, and 'thank you' when someone helps you.
grammar.commonMistake: 'Please' is often forgotten in a hurry. Pause, smile, and start the sentence with 'Please'.
grammar.memoryTip: Please opens the door and thank you closes it kindly.
grammar.practice: Ask politely three times: Please help me. Thank you. Please pass the bread. Thank you. Please open the door. Thank you.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Point to town places as an adult names them, then name them on your own. Say: 'There is a shop.' 'There is a school.' 'There is a mosque.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern There is a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Copy the adult, then describe your own little map. Say: 'The shop is next to the school.' 'The park is next to the market.' 'My home is next to the road.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern The ___ is next to the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Ask and answer about the people who help your town. Say: 'Who works at the hospital?' 'A doctor works at the hospital.' 'A baker works at the shop.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern A ___ works at the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Play traffic lights with a partner. Say: 'Red means stop.' 'Green means go.' 'Look left and right, then walk.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Stop. / Go. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Role-play a trip around your town. Say: 'I go to the market.' 'I go to the mosque on Friday.' 'I go to the park with Musa.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I go to the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Take your own turn buying something politely. Say: 'Please may I have some bread?' 'Thank you.' 'You are welcome.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Please ___. Thank you. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw your street with two places on it. Tell an adult who helps people in each one.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace 'shop' and 'mosque' twice. Draw a tiny door under each word, then point and read them to an adult.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match the words doctor, baker and teacher to their places. Then copy the word for the person who helps you learn.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your town map: 'road' and 'park'. Then trace the safe way from your home to school.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.promptAndInstructions: Copy and finish twice: 'I go to the ___.' Use market and mosque. Remember a full stop at the end of each one.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write the polite words 'Please' and 'Thank you' on a shopping card. Then write one sentence: 'Please may I have a ___.'
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

activities.title: Activity 1
activities.instructionsAndItems: Colour the town: the school blue, the market orange, the mosque green and the hospital white. Point and name each place as you finish.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2
activities.instructionsAndItems: Draw your favourite place in town. Then say: 'I go to the ___. I go with my ___.' Show your picture to the class.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 3
activities.instructionsAndItems: Colour the traffic light: the top red, the middle amber and the bottom green. Then say what each colour tells us to do.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 4
activities.instructionsAndItems: Practise crossing safely. 1. Stop at the kerb. 2. Look left. 3. Look right. 4. Listen, then walk. Say each step as you do it.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 5
activities.instructionsAndItems: Point to five places in the town picture and say 'I can see a ___' for each. Then choose the one nearest your home.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 6
activities.instructionsAndItems: Build a town from boxes. Make a shop, a school and a mosque. Walk a toy person between them and say where it is going.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

assignments.title: My City Places show-and-tell page
assignments.instructions: Draw a map of your town. 1) Draw four places: the shop, the market, the mosque and the hospital. 2) Label two of them with traced words. 3) Colour a traffic light red, yellow and green. 4) Record yourself saying: There is a ___. The shop is next to the ___. A doctor works at the hospital. Red means stop, green means go. 5) Finish politely: Please help me. Thank you.
assignments.submissionType: Drawing or object + labels + short audio/video + reflection

quizzes.question: Which word names a place in a town?
quizzes.explanation: A market is a place you can go to; a pencil is a thing, a goat is an animal and wet describes something.

quizzes.question: Where do you go if you are very ill and need a doctor?
quizzes.options: the school | the park | the shop | the hospital
quizzes.explanation: Doctors and nurses work at the hospital; we go to the park to play, the shop to buy and the school to learn.

quizzes.question: Where do Muslims go to pray together?
quizzes.options: the mosque | the park | the bus stop | the market
quizzes.explanation: The mosque is the place for prayer; the market, park and bus stop are for shopping, playing and waiting.

quizzes.question: Where do you go to buy bread and milk?
quizzes.options: the hospital | the shop | the park | the mosque
quizzes.explanation: We buy food at the shop; you cannot buy bread at a hospital, a park or a mosque.

quizzes.question: Where do you wait to get on a bus?
quizzes.options: the hospital | the school | the bus stop | the mosque
quizzes.explanation: Buses pick people up at the bus stop; the other places are not for waiting for a bus.

quizzes.question: Who looks after you when you are ill in hospital?
quizzes.options: a baker | a farmer | a driver | a doctor
quizzes.explanation: A doctor helps ill people; a farmer grows food, a driver drives and a baker makes bread.

quizzes.question: Who teaches the children at school?
quizzes.options: a teacher | a nurse | a pilot | a shopkeeper
quizzes.explanation: A teacher works at school; a nurse works at a hospital, a shopkeeper at a shop and a pilot flies a plane.

quizzes.question: The traffic light turns red. What must you do?
quizzes.explanation: Red means stop and wait; green is the light that means go.

quizzes.question: The park is right beside the school. Which sentence says this?
quizzes.options: The park is inside the school. | The school is in the park. | The park is next to the school. | The park is under the school.
quizzes.correctAnswer: The park is next to the school.
quizzes.explanation: Next to means beside; under, inside and in tell us something different.

quizzes.question: Which sentence tells where you are going?
quizzes.options: Market I go to. | I go the market. | I market to go. | I go to the market.
quizzes.explanation: We need the little word to before the place; the other sentences are missing it or muddled.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name places in a town (shop, market, school, hospital, mosque, park).
